hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as feature extractors within a graph neural network architecture that explicitly encodes gas identity and mixture composition as graph structure, then the resulting model will more accurately and generalizably predict mixture plasma etching rates across unseen process conditions and gas compositions compared to a standard feedforward neural network trained directly on mixture data, because the graph-based approach leverages transferable single-gas physical knowledge and relational inductive biases to capture inter-gas interactions in multi-component plasma systems.
